title: Add conditional release-checking system test
body:
Unfortunately on a number of occasions, Podman has been released
officially with a `-dev` suffix in the version number.  Assist in
catching this mistake at release time by the addition of a simple
conditional test.  Note that it must be positively enabled by a
magic env. var. before executing the system tests.

Also (thanks to @lsm5) update the TMT test to trigger the new
condition for future Fedora releases.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Isn't this already to late? The test must happen on the release commit and not in the gating env. I mean it can be in gating for sure but once it is tagged there is no going back so the release process should check that no?

We use github actions to build the release installers and publish the final release on tag push so I assume for the installer builds we could have a smoke check that runs podman --version (or podman-remote rather) and then abort publishing the release in case the wrong commit was tagged.
cc @ashley-cui

@Luap99 We do check a -dev suffix in our release action [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/87d88c3b09de493368b2633aeaea3ff0fc0badef/.github/workflows/release.yml#L41), but it doesn't check what's actually in the version.go file. It should be easy enough to add that in, we can just parse it, but like you said, this is a post-tag commit failsafe.

@ashley-cui Ack, yeah I think that is already good enough as failsafe.

I guess if the goal is to only have gating testing with TMT to test it then sure we can go ahead with it.

Ref.: [Example of new test failing](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6186711874535424/html/sys-podman-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html).

> I guess if the goal is to only have gating testing with TMT to test it then sure we can go ahead with it.

Thanks for the clarification @Luap99 and @ashley-cui I'm happy to hear there's another check for this in the release pipeline.

My intention here is to catch this problem in downstream gating, where the system tests run again.  I was also intending to backport this new test to the `-rhel` release branches where presumably the fancy release pipeline does *not* run(?).  But maybe this is all less necessary than having this test on Buildah/Skopeo (already merged) which lack the pipeline checks(?).

Edit: I think the existing release pipeline checks are probably fine for Fedora.  For RHEL though, we're building elsewhere and it's at least conceivable the spec points at a `-dev` commit on accident.  In that case, having this check as a stop-gap would be helpful.

I'm fine either way (merge or close).  There's likely more benefit in backporting this to the old `-rhel` releases (that don't use the github action) then for modern Fedora releases.  Even if this is closed, I can still take care of that.

presumably we will have TMT gating test in centos stream and RHEL 10 I think? So in that case it seems logical to gate them as well in the future.
As for older branches upstream it is a bit complicated https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26316

In any case there isn't any harm in merging this so LGTM

@lsm5 PTAL and merge if the TMT change is correct
